firstSection.title: An Engineer Bridging Both Backend and Frontend
firstSection.text:
Prior to 2016, I worked as a systems engineer. Back then, I found great fulfillment and enjoyment in exploring problems like a puzzle and experiencing the sense of accomplishment when uncovering the root cause.

However, focusing solely on the backend made me increasingly aware of design-related issues, such as 'the system feels sluggish' or 'the design looks outdated.' Desiring to improve not just the core system but also the user experience and design, I transitioned to web production in 2016.

As the sole engineer in my web production roles, I handled a wide range of responsibilities, including WordPress customization, technical support, and workflow optimization.

For development projects, I built websites from scratch based on design drafts provided by designers via Illustrator. During the maintenance and operation phase, I implemented changes based on instruction sheets, frequently executing updates even without existing design assets.

I have a natural interest in art; on my days off, I create personal artwork as a hobby and read ZINEs in search of fresh ideas.

Moving forward, I aim to leverage my comprehensive experience in both backend and frontend development while expanding into data utilization. Operating under more systematized processes and guidelines, I want to continuously learn while engaging in work I find genuinely exciting. Ultimately, I aspire to be an engineer who contributes to raising the technological baseline of organizations and society from a long-term perspective.

data.description: Taken at a time when my hearing difficulties and developmental disorders were undiagnosed, meaning the test was taken without accommodations. Reading comprehension for explicit information, understanding specifications, and fact-based English text processing were stable.

data.title: Data Science Mathematics Strategist - Intermediate Level

VisualThinking.text:
Here, as part of exploring my own roots, I have published an ethnography on my personal website regarding the 'Mixed-race orphan community and its cultural transition' in 1970s Nagoya, which my grandparents were involved with.
Precisely because it is a sensitive theme, I conducted the research with a strong emphasis on objectivity and multi-faceted perspectives, eliminating subjective bias.
When investigating cross-cultural and social conditions, my rigorous process involved using primary information from local users on platforms like Reddit and X (formerly Twitter) as a starting point, and cross-checking it against secondary sources like Wikipedia and existing academic research to ensure there were no information biases or errors.
Similarly, in historical research, I prioritized tracing back to public and primary records, such as old books and contemporary newspaper articles, checking for consistency by comparing multiple documents.
Through this research into post-war history and social welfare, I developed a perspective of viewing issues not merely as individual problems, but in terms of how social systems and infrastructure impact people's lives.
As an extension of this, I have come to want to be involved in work from a standpoint where I can observe how systems and infrastructure function and where challenges arise when emergencies or social disruptions occur, without being swayed by emotions or personal stances.

These skills in document organization and structural thinking are also utilized in my current web production work.
Even now, it is my habit to directly consult English articles and overseas primary sources during work-related research. While I feel less confident with spoken English conversation, I have no trouble handling information gathering and analysis focused on English reading comprehension for practical operations.
In operation, maintenance, and optimization tasks, I prioritize the accuracy of information by reviewing announcements from official Google accounts and existing questions on Stack Overflow, and referencing the latest official English documentation whenever uncertainties arise.

Additionally, I have personally engaged in illustration creation and image processing.
I have always loved creating things that evoke emotion in viewers or communicate intuitively through expression. Regardless of the format of the activity, I intend to continue exploring this kind of expression moving forward.
